2606.06418.title: Double Preconditioning (DoPr): Optimization for Test-Time Performance, not Validation Loss
2606.06418.paper_summary: Many modern applications of deep learning involve training a neural network via a one-step prediction loss (e.g., $L^2$ regression, cross-entropy), but deploy the network by rolling out along its own predictions. Key examples include autoregressive language modeling, flow-based generative modeling, and robot policy learning. It is well-documented that these settings induce a phenomenon we call test-time feedback (TTF): the mismatch between the training/validation loss and downstream metrics of interest, such as task success rate and generation quality, which grows with task length. While data curation, architecture, and objective design have been proposed to combat train-test shift in TTF settings, this paper proposes optimization as a new design axis to mitigate error accumulation. Specifically, we introduce a new optimization paradigm called double-preconditioning (DoPr) uniquely tailored to the challenges of TTF. DoPr combines gradient-wise preconditioning, as in Adam and Muon, with activation-wise preconditioning (AP), such as in KFAC. We show that the addition of AP yields a drop-in intervention for increasing downstream model performance across a range of TTF settings. Interestingly, these gains in test-time performance do not consistently accompany improvements in validation loss, opening new questions about how to properly evaluate models trained with one-step supervised objectives.
2606.06418.paper_summary_zh: 許多現代深度學習應用涉及通過一步預測損失（例如，$L^2$ 回歸、交叉熵）訓練神經網絡，但通過沿著自身預測進行展開來部署網絡。關鍵範例包括自回歸語言建模、基於流的生成建模和機器人策略學習。這些情境引發了一種現象，我們稱之為測試時反饋（TTF）：訓練/驗證損失與下游關注指標（如任務成功率和生成質量）之間的不匹配，隨著任務長度的增加而增長。雖然已提出數據策劃、架構和目標設計來應對 TTF 設定中的訓練-測試偏移，但本文提出優化作為一個新的設計方向來減輕錯誤累積。具體而言，我們引入了一種新的優化範式，稱為雙重預處理（DoPr），專門針對 TTF 的挑戰。DoPr 將逐梯度預處理（如 Adam 和 Muon）與激活逐預處理（AP，如 KFAC）相結合。我們展示了 AP 的增加為提高各種 TTF 設定下的下游模型性能提供了一種即時干預。值得注意的是，這些測試時性能的提升並不總是伴隨著驗證損失的改善，這引發了關於如何正確評估使用一步監督目標訓練的模型的新問題。
2606.06418.author: Thomas T. Zhang et.al.
2606.06418.authors: Thomas T. Zhang, Alok Shah, Yifei Zhang, Vincent Zhang, Nikolai Matni, Max Simchowitz
